---
title: "16S Microbiome Profiling Report"
subtitle: "Illumina Short-Read Sequencing (DADA2 / ASV)"
date: today
date-format: long

number-sections: true
highlight-style: pygments
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-title: Contents
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <style>
          /* Iframe containers — block layout, never clips content */
          .iframe-container {
            width: 100%;
            margin-bottom: 1.5em;
            border-radius: 8px;
            overflow: hidden;
            box-shadow: 0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);
          }
          .iframe-container iframe {
            display: block;
            width: 100%;
            height: 100%;  /* <--- ADD THIS LINE */
            min-height: 400px; /* Optional: Adds a safety net so it never collapses entirely */
            border: none;
            border-radius: 8px;
          }
          .dropdown-menu { z-index: 1050 !important; }
          .table thead th, table thead th {
            background-color: #2C3E50 !important;
            color: #ffffff !important;
            border-bottom: 2px solid #1A252F !important;
          }
          .btn-outline-primary {
            border-color: #3b82f6 !important;
            color: #3b82f6 !important;
            font-weight: 600 !important;
            padding: 8px 20px !important;
            border-radius: 30px !important;
            transition: all 0.2s ease-in-out !important;
            margin: 2px !important;
          }
          .btn-outline-primary:hover, .btn-outline-primary.active {
            background-color: #3b82f6 !important;
            color: #ffffff !important;
            box-shadow: 0 4px 12px rgba(59, 130, 246, 0.25) !important;
            border-color: #3b82f6 !important;
          }
          </style>
      - file: header_footer/header1.html
    include-after-body:
      - file: header_footer/footer1.html
    grid:
      body-width: 1200px
      sidebar-width: 258px
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: false
editor: visual
---

::: {#wrn-1 .callout-important}
## Important
Please keep this interactive report inside its designated folder so the linked interactive plots and data files continue to function properly.
:::

# Project Workflow

This report details the taxonomic composition, diversity metrics, and predicted functional pathways of microbial communities identified via **Illumina short-read 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing**. The pipeline uses the DADA2 framework to infer exact Amplicon Sequence Variants (ASVs) and assigns taxonomy against the SILVA 138.1 reference database. Click on any section to expand.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## 1. Quality Control & Preprocessing
* **Process:** Raw Illumina paired-end reads were quality-assessed using **FastQC** and primers/adapters removed using **Cutadapt**. Quality metrics were aggregated via **MultiQC**.
* **Technology:** Illumina paired-end 16S amplicon sequencing (typically V3-V4 or V4 region).
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## 2. ASV Inference & Taxonomy
* **Process:** The **DADA2** pipeline was used to filter, denoise, and merge paired-end reads to resolve exact Amplicon Sequence Variants (ASVs). Chimeras were removed and taxonomy was assigned against the **SILVA 138.1** reference database.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## 3. Phylogenetic Tree & Diversity Analysis
* **Process:** ASV sequences were aligned with **DECIPHER** and a phylogenetic tree was built using **FastTree** (GTR+CAT model). The tree was imported into **Phyloseq** to enable UniFrac diversity analysis alongside Bray-Curtis and Jaccard.
* **Alpha diversity** (Observed, Shannon, Simpson) was calculated per sample. **Beta diversity** (PCoA + NMDS) was visualized for all four distance metrics.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## 4. Biomarker Discovery & Functional Prediction
* **Differential Abundance:** **ANCOM-BC2** was applied at the **Genus** level to identify significantly differentially abundant taxa between experimental groups.
* **Functional Prediction:** **PICRUSt2** was used to predict MetaCyc metabolic pathways from the 16S ASV profiles by phylogenetic placement.
:::

# Bioinformatic Pipeline

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'themeVariables': { 'fontSize': '16px'}, 'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 40, 'rankSpacing': 50}}}%%
flowchart TD
    A[("Illumina FASTQ\nRaw Reads")]
    B["1. QC & Trimming<br/>(Cutadapt / FastQC<br/>MultiQC)"]
    C["2. ASV Denoising<br/>(DADA2)"]
    D["3. Taxonomy Assignment<br/>(SILVA 138.1)"]
    E["3b. Phylogenetic Tree<br/>(DECIPHER + FastTree)"]
    F{"4. Diversity Analysis<br/>(Phyloseq)"}
    G["5. Biomarker Discovery<br/>(ANCOM-BC2 @ Genus)"]
    H["6. Functional Prediction<br/>(PICRUSt2)"]
    I["7. Visualizations<br/>(Plotly / ggplot2)"]
    J[("Final Interactive<br/>Report")]

    A --> B --> C --> D & E --> F --> G & H --> I --> J

    classDef input fill:#2b2d42,color:#fff,stroke:none
    classDef process fill:#edf2f4,stroke:#8d99ae,color:#2b2d42
    classDef check fill:#ffddd2,stroke:#e29578,color:#2b2d42
    classDef output fill:#83c5be,stroke:#006d77,color:#fff,stroke-width:2px

    class A input
    class B,C,D,E,G,H,I process
    class F check
    class J output
```

# Quality Control
::: {.callout-tip}
Raw Illumina reads require quality control to remove low-quality bases and primer/adapter sequences. The MultiQC report aggregates FastQC and Cutadapt metrics across all samples.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="iframe-container">
  <iframe src="multiqc_report.html" title="MultiQC Report" data-external="1"></iframe>
</div>
<br>
<div class="dropdown" style="margin-top: 10px;">
  <button class="btn btn-primary dropdown-toggle" type="button" data-bs-toggle="dropdown" aria-expanded="false">
    <i class="bi bi-download"></i> Download QC Data
  </button>
  <ul class="dropdown-menu">
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="multiqc_report.html" download="multiqc_report.html">Full MultiQC HTML Report</a></li>
  </ul>
</div>
```

# Sample Properties & Sequencing Depth

::: {.callout-tip}
The table below details the experimental groups and metadata for all samples included in the final analysis, along with the total number of quality-filtered reads (Read Input) successfully retained and mapped to taxa.
:::

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
library(phyloseq)
library(DT)
library(dplyr)

rds_path <- "results/03_phyloseq/phyloseq_obj.rds"
if(!file.exists(rds_path)) rds_path <- "../results/03_phyloseq/phyloseq_obj.rds"

if(file.exists(rds_path)) {
  ps <- readRDS(rds_path)
  meta_df <- data.frame(sample_data(ps), check.names = FALSE)
  
  # Calculate Total Reads per sample
  meta_df$Total_Reads <- sample_sums(ps)
  
  # Format SampleID
  if(!"SampleID" %in% colnames(meta_df)) {
    meta_df <- cbind(SampleID = rownames(meta_df), meta_df)
  }
  
  # Render interactive table
  datatable(meta_df, 
            rownames = FALSE,
            class = 'display compact cell-border stripe',
            options = list(
              pageLength = 15, 
              scrollX = TRUE,
              dom = 'Bfrtip'
            ))
} else {
  cat("Sample metadata and read counts will be displayed here once the Phyloseq object is generated.")
}
```

# Taxonomic Composition
::: {.callout-tip}
Relative abundance of microbial taxa across samples. Use the tabs to switch between taxonomic ranks. Hover over bars for exact percentage values.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="d-flex justify-content-center flex-wrap gap-2 mb-4">
  <button class="btn btn-outline-primary active" onclick="switchTaxa('phylum', this)">Phylum</button>
  <button class="btn btn-outline-primary" onclick="switchTaxa('class', this)">Class</button>
  <button class="btn btn-outline-primary" onclick="switchTaxa('order', this)">Order</button>
  <button class="btn btn-outline-primary" onclick="switchTaxa('family', this)">Family</button>
  <button class="btn btn-outline-primary" onclick="switchTaxa('genus', this)">Genus</button>
  <button class="btn btn-outline-primary" onclick="switchTaxa('species', this)">Species</button>
</div>

<div class="iframe-container" style="height: 850px;">
  <iframe id="taxa-iframe" src="interactive_reports/01_taxa_phylum.html" title="Taxonomic Composition" data-external="1"></iframe>
</div>

<script>
function switchTaxa(level, btn) {
  document.getElementById('taxa-iframe').src = `interactive_reports/01_taxa_${level}.html`;
  btn.parentElement.querySelectorAll('button').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active'));
  btn.classList.add('active');
}
</script>
```

```{=html}
<br>
<div class="dropdown">
  <button class="btn btn-primary dropdown-toggle" type="button" data-bs-toggle="dropdown" aria-expanded="false">
    <i class="bi bi-download"></i> Download Taxonomic Abundance Data
  </button>
  <ul class="dropdown-menu">
    <li><h6 class="dropdown-header">Raw Count Tables (TSV)</h6></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="03_phyloseq/abundance_phylum.tsv" download="abundance_phylum.tsv">Phylum Level</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="03_phyloseq/abundance_class.tsv" download="abundance_class.tsv">Class Level</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="03_phyloseq/abundance_order.tsv" download="abundance_order.tsv">Order Level</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="03_phyloseq/abundance_family.tsv" download="abundance_family.tsv">Family Level</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="03_phyloseq/abundance_genus.tsv" download="abundance_genus.tsv">Genus Level</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="03_phyloseq/abundance_species.tsv" download="abundance_species.tsv">Species Level</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="03_phyloseq/abundance_asv.tsv" download="abundance_asv.tsv">Raw ASV Level</a></li>
    <li><hr class="dropdown-divider"></li>
    <li><h6 class="dropdown-header">Combined Format</h6></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="03_phyloseq/taxa_abundance_tables.xlsx" download="taxa_abundance_tables.xlsx">All Levels (Excel Workbook)</a></li>
  </ul>
</div>
```

# Rarefaction Analysis
::: {.callout-tip}
Rarefaction curves show whether sequencing depth was sufficient to capture the full microbial diversity. Plateauing curves indicate sufficient sequencing depth. ASV-level curves reflect the highest resolution of the DADA2 pipeline.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="d-flex justify-content-center flex-wrap gap-2 mb-4">
  <button class="btn btn-outline-primary active" onclick="switchRare('asv_observed', this)">ASV-Level Richness</button>
  <button class="btn btn-outline-primary" onclick="switchRare('genus_observed', this)">Genus-Level Richness</button>
  <button class="btn btn-outline-primary" onclick="switchRare('asv_shannon', this)">Shannon Saturation</button>
</div>

<div class="iframe-container" style="height: 750px;">
  <iframe id="rare-iframe" src="interactive_reports/02b_rarefaction_asv_observed.html" title="Rarefaction Curves" data-external="1"></iframe>
</div>

<script>
function switchRare(metric, btn) {
  document.getElementById('rare-iframe').src = `interactive_reports/02b_rarefaction_${metric}.html`;
  btn.parentElement.querySelectorAll('button').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active'));
  btn.classList.add('active');
}
</script>
```

# Alpha Diversity (Within-Sample)
::: {.callout-tip}
Alpha diversity measures the complexity within a single sample. **Observed** richness counts unique ASVs; **Shannon** accounts for evenness; **Simpson** weights dominant taxa. Statistical comparisons use Kruskal-Wallis with post-hoc Wilcoxon tests.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="iframe-container" style="height: 750px;">
  <iframe src="interactive_reports/02_alpha_diversity_with_table.html" title="Alpha Diversity" data-external="1"></iframe>
</div>
<br>
<div class="dropdown" style="margin-top: 10px;">
  <button class="btn btn-primary dropdown-toggle" type="button" data-bs-toggle="dropdown" aria-expanded="false">
    <i class="bi bi-download"></i> Download Alpha Diversity Data
  </button>
  <ul class="dropdown-menu">
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/alpha/alpha_diversity_table.tsv" download="alpha_diversity_table.tsv">Alpha Diversity Metrics (TSV)</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/alpha/kruskal_observed.tsv" download="kruskal_observed.tsv">Kruskal-Wallis — Observed (TSV)</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/alpha/kruskal_shannon.tsv" download="kruskal_shannon.tsv">Kruskal-Wallis — Shannon (TSV)</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/alpha/pairwise_wilcoxon_shannon.tsv" download="pairwise_wilcoxon_shannon.tsv">Pairwise Wilcoxon (TSV)</a></li>
  </ul>
</div>
```

# Beta Diversity (Between-Sample)
::: {.callout-tip}
Beta diversity measures structural differences between communities. **Bray-Curtis** and **Jaccard** capture abundance-based and presence/absence dissimilarity; **Weighted/Unweighted UniFrac** incorporate phylogenetic distances (available because a tree was built from the ASVs). Significance tested by **PERMANOVA** and assumptions validated by **PERMDISP**.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="iframe-container" style="height: 1000px;">
  <iframe src="interactive_reports/03_beta_diversity_with_stats.html" title="Beta Diversity Analysis" data-external="1"></iframe>
</div>
<br>
<div class="dropdown" style="margin-top: 10px;">
  <button class="btn btn-primary dropdown-toggle" type="button" data-bs-toggle="dropdown" aria-expanded="false">
    <i class="bi bi-download"></i> Download Beta Diversity Stats & Plots
  </button>
  <ul class="dropdown-menu">
    <li><h6 class="dropdown-header">Statistical Tables</h6></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/beta/permanova_bray_curtis.tsv" download>PERMANOVA - Bray-Curtis (TSV)</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/beta/permanova_jaccard.tsv" download>PERMANOVA - Jaccard (TSV)</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/beta/permanova_weighted_unifrac.tsv" download>PERMANOVA - Weighted UniFrac (TSV)</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/beta/permanova_unweighted_unifrac.tsv" download>PERMANOVA - Unweighted UniFrac (TSV)</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/beta/permdisp_bray_curtis.tsv" download>PERMDISP - Bray-Curtis (TSV)</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/beta/permdisp_jaccard.tsv" download>PERMDISP - Jaccard (TSV)</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/beta/permdisp_weighted_unifrac.tsv" download>PERMDISP - Weighted UniFrac (TSV)</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/beta/permdisp_unweighted_unifrac.tsv" download>PERMDISP - Unweighted UniFrac (TSV)</a></li>
    <li><hr class="dropdown-divider"></li>
    <li><h6 class="dropdown-header">High-Res Ordination Plots</h6></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/beta/pcoa_bray_curtis.png" download>PCoA - Bray-Curtis (PNG)</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/beta/nmds_bray_curtis.png" download>NMDS - Bray-Curtis (PNG)</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/beta/pcoa_jaccard.png" download>PCoA - Jaccard (PNG)</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/beta/nmds_jaccard.png" download>NMDS - Jaccard (PNG)</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/beta/pcoa_weighted_unifrac.png" download>PCoA - Weighted UniFrac (PNG)</a></li>
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/beta/pcoa_unweighted_unifrac.png" download>PCoA - Unweighted UniFrac (PNG)</a></li>
  </ul>
</div>
```

# Differential Abundance — Genus-Level Biomarkers
::: {.callout-important}
This section highlights microbial **genera** significantly differentially abundant between experimental cohorts. Statistical testing used **ANCOM-BC2** (Analysis of Compositions of Microbiomes with Bias Correction 2) — a rigorous compositional method. Use the tabs to switch between the Multi-Group and Pairwise Volcano views.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="d-flex justify-content-center flex-wrap gap-2 mb-4">
  <button class="btn btn-outline-primary active" onclick="switchDiff('multi', this)">Global Multi-Group Plot</button>
  <button class="btn btn-outline-primary" onclick="switchDiff('pairwise', this)">Pairwise Volcano Plot</button>
</div>

<div class="iframe-container" style="height: 800px;">
  <iframe id="diff-iframe" src="interactive_reports/04_diff_multi.html" title="Differential Biomarkers" data-external="1"></iframe>
</div>
<br>

<div id="diff-download-multi" class="dropdown" style="margin-top: 10px; display: block;">
  <button class="btn btn-primary dropdown-toggle" type="button" data-bs-toggle="dropdown" aria-expanded="false">
    <i class="bi bi-download"></i> Download ANCOM-BC2 Biomarker Data
  </button>
  <ul class="dropdown-menu">
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/differential/differential_abundance_species.tsv" download>Multi-Group ANCOM-BC2 Results (TSV)</a></li>
  </ul>
</div>

<div id="diff-download-pairwise" class="dropdown" style="margin-top: 10px; display: none;">
  <button class="btn btn-primary dropdown-toggle" type="button" data-bs-toggle="dropdown" aria-expanded="false">
    <i class="bi bi-download"></i> Download Pairwise Results
  </button>
  <ul class="dropdown-menu">
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="04_stats/differential/pairwise_differential_abundance.tsv" download>Pairwise ANCOM-BC2 Results (TSV)</a></li>
  </ul>
</div>

<script>
function switchDiff(view, btn) {
  document.getElementById('diff-iframe').src = `interactive_reports/04_diff_${view}.html`;
  document.getElementById('diff-download-multi').style.display = view === 'multi' ? 'block' : 'none';
  document.getElementById('diff-download-pairwise').style.display = view === 'pairwise' ? 'block' : 'none';
  btn.parentElement.querySelectorAll('button').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active'));
  btn.classList.add('active');
}
</script>
```

# Functional Prediction (PICRUSt2)
::: {.callout-tip}
**PICRUSt2** predicts MetaCyc metabolic pathways from the 16S ASV profile using phylogenetic placement. The heatmap shows the top most variable pathways across samples, illustrating functional shifts between communities.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="iframe-container" style="height: 600px;">
  <iframe src="interactive_reports/05_functional_pathways.html" title="Functional Pathways Heatmap" data-external="1"></iframe>
</div>
<br>
<div class="dropdown" style="margin-top: 10px;">
  <button class="btn btn-primary dropdown-toggle" type="button" data-bs-toggle="dropdown" aria-expanded="false">
    <i class="bi bi-download"></i> Download Functional Data
  </button>
  <ul class="dropdown-menu">
    <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="06_picrust2/pathways_out/path_abun_unstrat.tsv" download="path_abun_unstrat.tsv">Raw Pathway Abundance (TSV)</a></li>
  </ul>
</div>
```

# References

Callahan, B. J., McMurdie, P. J., Rosen, M. J., Han, A. W., Johnson, A. J., & Holmes, S. P. (2016). DADA2: High-resolution sample inference from Illumina amplicon data. *Nature Methods*, 13(7), 581–583. https://doi.org/10.1038/nmeth.3869

Douglas, G. M., Maffei, V. J., Zaneveld, J. R., Yurgel, S. N., Brown, J. R., Taylor, C. M., ... & Langille, M. G. (2020). PICRUSt2 for prediction of metagenome functions. *Nature Biotechnology*, 38(6), 685–688. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41587-020-0548-6

Ewels, P., Magnusson, M., Lundin, S., & Käller, M. (2016). MultiQC: summarize analysis results for multiple tools and samples in a single report. *Bioinformatics*, 32(19), 3047–3048. https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btw354

Lin, H., & Peddada, S. D. (2020). Analysis of compositions of microbiomes with bias correction. *Nature Communications*, 11, 3514. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-020-17041-7

Martin, M. (2011). Cutadapt removes adapter sequences from high-throughput sequencing reads. *EMBnet.journal*, 17(1), 10–12. https://doi.org/10.14806/ej.17.1.200

McMurdie, P. J., & Holmes, S. (2013). phyloseq: an R package for reproducible interactive analysis and graphics of microbiome census data. *PLoS One*, 8(4), e61217. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0061217

Oksanen, J., Blanchet, F. G., Friendly, M., Kindt, R., Legendre, P., McGlinn, D., ... & Wagner, H. (2020). vegan: Community Ecology Package. https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=vegan

Price, M. N., Dehal, P. S., & Arkin, A. P. (2010). FastTree 2 — Approximately maximum-likelihood trees for large alignments. *PLoS One*, 5(3), e9490. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0009490

Wright, E. S. (2016). Using DECIPHER v2.0 to Analyze Big Biological Sequence Data in R. *The R Journal*, 8(1), 352–359. https://doi.org/10.32614/RJ-2016-025

# Software and Database Versions

All tools managed via a locked **Pixi** (Conda/Bioconda/CRAN) environment:

| Software / Package | Version | Purpose |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **FastQC** | v0.12.1+ | Raw read quality assessment |
| **Cutadapt** | v5.2+ | Primer and adapter trimming |
| **MultiQC** | v1.35+ | Quality control report aggregation |
| **DADA2** (R) | v1.38.0+ | ASV inference, denoising, chimera removal |
| **DECIPHER** (R) | v3.6.0+ | Multiple sequence alignment for phylogeny |
| **FastTree** | v2.2.0+ | Maximum-likelihood phylogenetic tree building |
| **Phyloseq** (R) | v1.54.0+ | Microbiome data handling and diversity metrics |
| **ANCOM-BC2** (R) | v2.12.0+ | Differential abundance testing at Genus level |
| **Vegan** (R) | v2.7-3+ | PERMANOVA, PERMDISP, ecological diversity |
| **PICRUSt2** | v2.6.3+ | Metagenomic functional pathway prediction |
| **Plotly** (R) | v4.10.4+ | Interactive HTML data visualization |
| **Quarto** | v1.4+ | Scientific report generation and rendering |

: {.striped .hover}

*Note: Taxonomy was assigned using the **SILVA 138.1** reference database for 16S rRNA sequences. Functional predictions were mapped against the **MetaCyc** metabolic pathway database via PICRUSt2.*
